description_long: This object is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a compact fuselage and a single vertical stabilizer at the tail. The wings are mounted low on the fuselage and appear to be straight with no visible sweep or winglets. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed, with a clear canopy or window section. The airplane features a tricycle landing gear configuration, with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, all of which appear to be fixed. The engine is not prominently visible, but the design suggests a single-engine configuration, likely mounted at the nose. The exterior is primarily gray with some colored markings and panels, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A small, gray, fixed-wing airplane with a low-mounted straight wing, single vertical tail, and tricycle landing gear. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front of the fuselage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit canopy, and the low-mounted wings extending to either side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the cockpit placement, and the tricycle landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear-side view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, tail section, and the straight wing configuration.
description_view_3: Opposite front view showing the nose, cockpit, and the symmetrical wing and landing gear arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle, fixed landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy.
